name: Pyridoxine (Vitamin B6)

sources.title: Vitamin B6 Fact Sheet for Health Professionals (NIH ODS)

description: Vitamin B6 forms support amino-acid and neurotransmitter metabolism; RDA is low mg/day range.
summary: Essential B6 cofactor nutrient; prolonged high-dose supplementation can cause neuropathy.
mechanism: B6 enzymes support transamination/decarboxylation and neurotransmitter pathways; non-deficiency benefits are mixed.
safetyNotes: Chronic high doses can cause sensory neuropathy.
interactions: High-dose B6 may affect phenytoin and levodopa response.
dosage: Adult RDA about 1.3–1.7 mg/day; common supplemental studies use 50–100 mg/day.
preparation: Pyridoxine HCl tablets/capsules, often in B-complex products.
regulatoryStatus: Essential vitamin with dietary reference intakes.
mechanisms: B6 enzymes support transamination/decarboxylation and neurotransmitter pathways; non-deficiency benefits are mixed
